title: Cursor and Visual selections reveal raw source
summary: The cursor row and every row in a characterwise, linewise, or blockwise Visual selection display source-faithful text; rows restore immediately when they leave the reveal set.
details: The reveal set is calculated from the current buffer cursor, current mode, and the Visual anchor.
Entering, extending, shrinking, swapping, or leaving a Visual selection is a presentation-state transition even when the changedtick is unchanged.
Every character-changing renderer must clear decorations on rows entering the reveal set and restore decorations on rows leaving it.
A row remaining under the cursor after Escape remains raw because cursor reveal still applies; all other formerly selected rows restore immediately.
rationale: Selections, edits, copying, and precise cursor work must operate against unambiguous source text rather than shifted display columns.

provenance.note: Owner confirmed that Visual selection should reveal every selected row and Escape should restore presentation immediately.
The shared helper defines cursor and Visual selection rows.

evidence.claim: Character, line, and block selections plus a cross-module matrix verify raw reveal and immediate restoration.

questions: What should happen to virtual text during Visual block selection?
Which rows restore after pressing Escape?
Why does touching a stale row with j or k repair it?

last_verified.method: Real headless mode transitions across all three Visual modes and representative renderers.
